LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LCD INCORPORATED, RICHARD HANSEN AND LCD'S MOTION TO STRIKE (COMPLAINT). THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - COURT DENIES MOTION TO STRIKE TREBLE DAMAGES. C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AUTHORIZES SUCH DAMAGES. STRIKE B. & P. SECTION 7160 AS A BASIS FOR RECOVERY. DENY AS TO ATTORNEY'S FEES BASED ON C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AND C.C.P. SECTION 1021.5. DENY MOTION TO STRIKE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SEVERAL BASIS FOR FUTURE AND SPECIAL DAMAGES. GRANT AS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DENY AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. IN ADDITION, THE COURT CLARIFIED TWO POINTS IN THE TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION TO STRIKE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF APPLIES ONLY TO PARAGRAPH 49 OF THE COMPLAINT, AND THE DENIAL OF THE MOTION TO STRIKE ATTORNEYS FEES PREMISED ON CCP 1029.8 IS AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND ON CCP 1021.5 IS AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LCD INCORPORATED, RICHARD HANSEN AND LCD'S MOTION TO STRIKE (COMPLAINT).
APPEARANCES: Barry K. Tagawa, Esq. of The Law Office of Barry K. Tagawa 951-8600 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret M. Farley, Esq. of Farley Law Offices 492-8690 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - COURT DENIES MOTION TO STRIKE TREBLE DAMAGES. C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AUTHORIZES SUCH DAMAGES. STRIKE B. & P. SECTION 7160 AS A BASIS FOR RECOVERY. DENY AS TO ATTORNEY'S FEES BASED ON C.C.P. SECTION 1029.8 AND C.C.P. SECTION 1021.5. DENY MOTION TO STRIKE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SEVERAL BASIS FOR FUTURE AND SPECIAL DAMAGES. GRANT AS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DENY AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. In addition, the Court clarified two points in the tentative ruling: The motion to strike injunctive relief applies only to paragraph 49 of the complaint, and the denial of the motion to strike attorneys fees premised on CCP 1029.8 is as to the first through eighth causes of action and on CCP 1021.5 is as to the ninth cause of action. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LCD INCORPORATED, RICHARD G. HANSEN AND LCD'S DEMURRER (TO COMPLAINT).
APPEARANCES: Barry K. Tagawa, Esq. of The Law Office of Barry K. Tagawa 951-8600 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret M. Farley, Esq. of Farley Law Offices 492-8690 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED. FACTS ARE ALLEGED TO SUPPORT SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. B. & P. CODE SECTION 7031 (B), 7028.4 CREATE A PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION. B. & P. CODE SECTION 5536 AND C.C.P SECTION 1029.8 PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A B. & P. SECTION 17200 CLAIM. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
